findings.message: loadItemsQuietly() catches the fetchItems() rejection and returns [] without rethrowing or emitting any telemetry. The RUM js-errors counter only increments from the window 'error'/'unhandledrejection' listeners wired in observability/rum.ts; swallowing the promise rejection here means no unhandledrejection fires, the counter never moves, and the failed /items load is invisible to RUM. It also removes the prior setError(true) path, so the user-facing error state is dead too — the failure is unobserved end to end.
findings.suggestion: Do not swallow the error. Either let it reject so the unhandledrejection listener records it (e.g. fetchItems().then(setItems).catch(() => setError(true)) and rethrow/report), or in the catch block explicitly emit a RUM error event before returning a fallback so the js-errors counter still increments.
